name: Validator
comment:
Validators work in a pipeline (or applicative functor style), similar to the one used in
json-decode-pipeline. Values are checked, and applied one by one a function.
If everything goes well, the pipeline returns an `Ok` result, otherwise it will return all the errors.

    Ok ValidatedForm
        |> validate (String.notEmpty "name is required") form.name
        |> validate (String.isEmail "email is invalid") form.email
        |> validateMany
            [ String.hasLetter "password needs to have letters"
            , String.hasNumber "password needs to have numbers"
            ]
            form.password
        |> noCheck form.message
        |> checkOnly Bool.isTrue form.approved

Errors will be accumulated from top to bottom into a List. If you want to know exactly which field had
errors, take a look at the `Validator.Named` module.

@docs Validated, Validator


# Pipeline functions

@docs noCheck, validate, checkOnly, validateMany, validateAll


# Helpers

@docs map, andThen, many, all, customValidator

aliases.name: Validated
aliases.comment:
`Validated` is simply an alias for a Result type, with errors as a list of string.
You will not need to work with some exotic type, it is only here for convenience.

aliases.name: Validator
aliases.comment: `Validator` is function, that checks a value, and returns a `Validated`. Some validators can change the type of the value, so the two type parameters are represent the input and the output types.

values.name: all
values.comment: Compose a list of validators for a single value. Checks are performed from left to right, and will return all errors.
values.type: List.List (Validator.Validator a a) -> Validator.Validator a a

values.name: andThen
values.comment:
Chain together validators. Only the first error will be returned on failure.

Note: `validateMany` and `validateAll` provide a cleaner interface for most use cases.
values.type: Validator.Validator b c -> Validator.Validator a b -> a -> Validator.Validated c

values.name: checkOnly
values.comment: Validate a value without applying it to the pipe.
values.type: Validator.Validator a b -> a -> Validator.Validated c -> Validator.Validated c

values.name: customValidator
values.comment: Create a custom validator, using a predicate function.

values.name: many
values.comment: Compose a list of validators for a single value. Checks are performed from left to right, and will stop on the first failure, returning only the first error.
values.type: List.List (Validator.Validator a a) -> Validator.Validator a a

values.name: map
values.comment: Map a function into the happy path.
values.type: (b -> c) -> Validator.Validator a b -> a -> Validator.Validated c

values.name: noCheck
values.comment: Pipe a value through without perfoming any checks.

values.name: validate
values.comment: Validate a value using a validator.
values.type: Validator.Validator a b -> a -> Validator.Validated (b -> c) -> Validator.Validated c

values.name: validateAll
values.comment:
Validate a value using a list of validators. Checks are performed from left to right, and will return all errors.

Note: `validateAll` is a convenience function onver `validate << all`
values.type: List.List (Validator.Validator a a) -> a -> Validator.Validated (a -> b) -> Validator.Validated b

values.name: validateMany
values.comment:
Validate a value using a list of validators. Checks are performed from left to right, and will stop on the first failure, returning only the first error.

Note: `validateMany` is a convenience function onver `validate << many`
values.type: List.List (Validator.Validator a a) -> a -> Validator.Validated (a -> b) -> Validator.Validated b

values.name: isFalse
values.comment: Checks if a boolean is false

values.name: isTrue
values.comment: Checks if a boolean is true

name: Validator.List
comment:
Validators for Lists.

@docs minLength, maxLength, every

values.name: every
values.comment:
Checks if every item in a list passes the validation.

    List.every (String.letterOnly "All items must be letters only") [ "abc", "def" ] == Ok [ "abc", "def" ]

    List.every (String.letterOnly "All items must be letters only") [ "abc", "ab2c" ] == Err [ "All items must be letters only" ]
values.type: Validator.Validator a a -> Validator.Validator (List.List a) (List.List a)

values.name: maxLength
values.comment: Checks if a list is shorter than or equal to a given number

values.name: minLength
values.comment: Checks if a list is longer than or equal to a given number

name: Validator.Maybe
comment:
Validators and helpers for Maybes.

@docs isJust, notRequired

values.name: isJust
values.comment:
Checks if a Maybe has a value, and if so, returns it.

If you need to check the value itself, you can use andThen.

    isJust "This field is required" myValue
        |> andThen (Validator.String.isEmail "This is not a valid email")

values.name: notRequired
values.comment:
This helper can be used to check the correctness of a non required Maybe.
It will only pass if the Maybe is Nothing, or if the value of Just passes the given validator.

    notRequired (Validator.String.isEmail "This is not a valid email") Nothing == Ok Nothing

    notRequired (Validator.String.isEmail "This is not a valid email") (Just "test@example.com") == Ok (Just "test@example.com")

    notRequired (Validator.String.isEmail "This is not a valid email") (Just "test") == Err [ "This is not a valid email" ]
values.type: Validator.Validator a a -> Maybe.Maybe a -> Validator.Validated (Maybe.Maybe a)

name: Validator.Named
comment:
Named validators work exactly the same way, as the simple ones, but every validate function takes a name string.

    Ok ValidatedForm
        |> validate "name" (notEmpty "name is required") form.name
        |> validate "email" (isEmail "email is invalid") form.email
        |> validateMany "password"
            [ hasLetter "password needs to have letters"
            , hasNumber "password needs to have numbers"
            ]
            form.password
        |> noCheck form.message
        |> checkOnly "approved" Bool.isTrue form.approved

Errors will be accumulated from top to bottom into a Dict, where the key is the field name.


# Pipeline functions

@docs noCheck, validate, checkOnly, validateMany, validateAll


# Errors

Named validators return lists of errors in a Dict, where the key is the field name. You don't even
need to use these helpers to get errors for a field, you can simply use `Dict.get FIELDNAME`.

@docs Validated, Errors, hasErrorsOn, getErrors, countErrors

aliases.name: Errors
aliases.comment: Named validators return lists of errors in a Dict, where the key is the field name.

aliases.name: Validated
aliases.comment: `Validated` is an alias for a Result type with Errors.

values.name: checkOnly
values.comment: Validate a value without applying it to the pipe.
values.type: String.String -> Validator.Validator a b -> a -> Validator.Named.Validated c -> Validator.Named.Validated c

values.name: countErrors
values.comment: Count all the errors.

values.name: getErrors
values.comment: Get errors for a given field.

values.name: hasErrorsOn
values.comment: Checks if there are any errors for a given field name.

values.name: noCheck
values.comment: Pipe a value through without perfoming any checks.
values.type: a -> Result.Result Validator.Named.Errors (a -> b) -> Result.Result Validator.Named.Errors b

values.name: validate
values.comment: Validate a value using a validator.
values.type: String.String -> Validator.Validator a b -> a -> Result.Result Validator.Named.Errors (b -> c) -> Result.Result Validator.Named.Errors c

values.name: validateAll
values.comment: Validate a value using a list of validators. Checks are performed from left to right, and will return all errors.
values.type: String.String -> List.List (Validator.Validator a a) -> a -> Result.Result Validator.Named.Errors (a -> b) -> Result.Result Validator.Named.Errors b

values.name: validateMany
values.comment: Validate a value using a list of validators. Checks are performed from left to right, and will stop on the first failure, returning only the first error.
values.type: String.String -> List.List (Validator.Validator a a) -> a -> Result.Result Validator.Named.Errors (a -> b) -> Result.Result Validator.Named.Errors b

name: Validator.Number
comment:
Validators for Ints and Floats.

@docs min, max

values.name: max
values.comment: Checks if a number is smaller than or equal to a given number

values.name: min
values.comment: Checks if a number is greater than or equal to a given number

name: Validator.String

values.name: hasLetter
values.comment: Checks if a string contains at least one letter.

values.name: hasNumber
values.comment: Checks if a string contains at least one number.

values.name: isEmail
values.comment:
Checks if a string is valid email.
This validator works for most emails, but it is not 100%.

Regex is from: <https://emailregex.com/>

values.name: isFloat
values.comment: Checks if a string can be casted to a float and if so, it returns the value.

values.name: isInt
values.comment: Checks if a string can be casted to an integer, and if so, it returns the value.

values.name: isPhoneJp
values.comment:
Checks if a string is a valid Japanese phone number.

Note: I only added japanese regex, because that's what I use, but you can easily use your own regex, or send me a Pull Request.

values.name: isUrl
values.comment:
Checks if a string is valid URL.

Regex is from: <https://gist.github.com/dperini/729294>

values.name: letterOnly
values.comment: Checks if a string only contains letters (white spaces are not allowed).

values.name: maxLength
values.comment: Checks if a string is shorter than or equal to a given value.

values.name: minLength
values.comment: Checks if a string is longer than or equal to a given value.

values.name: notBlank
values.comment: Checks if a string is empty (white spaces are not allowed).

values.name: notEmpty
values.comment: Checks if a string is empty (white spaces allowed).

values.name: numberOnly
values.comment: Checks if a string only contains numbers.

values.name: regexValidator
values.comment: Custom string validator using a regular expression.
values.type: String.String -> Maybe.Maybe Regex.Regex -> String.String -> Validator.Validated String.String
